**Clinical Reasoning:**
The pulp of teeth is innervated by sensory afferent fibers originating from the maxillary and mandibular divisions of the trigeminal nerve (cranial nerve V). While the facial nerve contributes to taste sensation in the anterior two-thirds of the tongue via the chorda tympani, the primary sensory innervation of dental pulp—responsible for pain and temperature detection—is directly attributable to the trigeminal nerve's branches.
